questions.question: What is natural vegetation?
questions.correct_answer: Plant community that grows naturally without human aid and remains undisturbed for a long time
questions.explanation: Natural vegetation refers to plants that grow naturally according to climate and soil conditions without human interference.

questions.question: What does the term flora mean?
questions.correct_answer: Plant life of a particular region
questions.explanation: Flora includes all plant species found in a specific geographical area.

questions.question: What does the term fauna mean?
questions.correct_answer: Animal life of a particular region
questions.explanation: Fauna refers to the variety of animal species found in a particular region.

questions.question: Which type of forest is found in areas receiving more than 200 cm of rainfall?
questions.explanation: These forests grow in regions with heavy rainfall and remain green throughout the year.

questions.question: Which forests are also known as monsoon forests?
questions.explanation: These forests shed their leaves during the dry season and are the most widespread forests in India.

questions.question: Which type of vegetation is found in regions receiving less than 70 cm rainfall?
questions.explanation: These forests grow in semi-arid regions and consist of thorny trees and shrubs.

questions.question: What are mangrove forests?
questions.correct_answer: Forests found in coastal tidal areas where plants grow in saline water
questions.explanation: Mangrove forests develop in river deltas and coastal regions influenced by tides.

questions.question: Which famous animal is found in the mangrove forests of the Sundarbans?
questions.explanation: The Sundarbans mangrove forests are the natural habitat of the Royal Bengal Tiger.

questions.question: What are biosphere reserves?
questions.correct_answer: Protected areas created to conserve biodiversity
questions.explanation: Biosphere reserves help protect plants, animals and ecosystems from human exploitation.

questions.question: When was the Wildlife Protection Act implemented in India?
questions.explanation: The Wildlife Protection Act was introduced to protect wildlife and prevent illegal hunting.

notes.title: Types of Natural Vegetation
notes.content: India has five major types of natural vegetation: Tropical Evergreen Forests, Tropical Deciduous Forests, Thorn Forests and Scrubs, Montane Forests, and Mangrove Forests.

notes.title: Importance of Forests
notes.content: Forests provide habitat for wildlife, help maintain ecological balance, and supply valuable resources such as timber, medicines and food.

notes.title: Wildlife Diversity in India
notes.content: India has approximately 90,000 animal species including mammals, birds, reptiles and fish.

notes.title: Threats to Biodiversity
notes.content: Major threats to biodiversity include deforestation, hunting, pollution, and introduction of alien species.

notes.title: Government Conservation Measures
notes.content: The government has established biosphere reserves, national parks and wildlife sanctuaries to protect natural ecosystems.

facts.fact: India is one of the 12 mega biodiversity countries in the world.
India has about 47,000 plant species and around 15,000 flowering plants.
The Sundarbans is the largest mangrove forest in India.
Project Tiger was launched to protect the declining tiger population in India.
